summary: All 33 TDD-anchor tests pass and the overall coverage is strong. Five findings identified: two are medium-severity (structural weaknesses that would survive against broken implementations), two are low-severity (duplicate test and vacuously-true runtime assertion), and one is low (missing exact boundary case). No critical or high issues. The suite may be approved after fixing the two medium findings.

findings.location: settle.test.ts — 'settle failure does not call any NonceStore mutation'
findings.title: NonceStore spy is disconnected from settleOnChain — runtime assertion is vacuously true
findings.description: The test creates `const store = new NonceStore()` and spies on `store.insert`, but `store` is never passed into `settleOnChain` (it does not appear in `makeOpts`). The spy will never be called regardless of what `settle.ts` does. The assertion `expect(insertSpy).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` passes even if `settle.ts` imported and called NonceStore. The test's only real value is the static grep at the end.
findings.details: The task spec anchor reads: 'mock a NonceStore spy; run verify (inserts entry) then settle (returns failure); assert nonceStore.delete/remove was never called by settle.' The intended test was a round-trip: call verify first (which inserts), then call settle with the same NonceStore, then assert settle never touched it. The implementation skips the verify step entirely.

replacement.approach: Wire a real NonceStore through both verify and settle in the same test.
replacement.note: The static-grep portion of the current test is still valuable and should be retained.

findings.location: verify.test.ts — 'nonce store has-and-insert is synchronous (no await between)'
findings.title: Microtask sentinel test does not actually detect an await between has and insert
findings.description: The test schedules a `Promise.resolve().then(() => order.push('microtask-pre'))` BEFORE calling `verifyEip3009Authorization`. Because the test `await`s that Promise first, 'microtask-pre' lands at index 0 before verify runs at all. There are no microtasks queued between the has and insert calls inside verify. If someone introduced `await somethingAsync()` between `nonceStore.has()` and `nonceStore.insert()` in verify.ts, the order array would still show `['microtask-pre', 'has', 'insert']` — the assertion `insertIdx === hasIdx + 1` would still pass because no pending microtask exists to interleave.
findings.details: The test asserts adjacency in the order array but cannot catch a true await-between-calls because no microtask is *pending* at the moment has fires. A correct sentinel must queue a microtask AFTER has fires (e.g. inject it inside the has spy implementation itself).

replacement.approach: Queue the sentinel microtask from inside the has spy, not before the outer await.
replacement.note: This is the only test style that would actually catch an inadvertent `await` being added between has and insert in future refactors.

findings.title: 'undecoded receipt revert falls back to receipt_reverted' is an exact duplicate of 'receipt status reverted'
findings.description: Both tests set `waitForTransactionReceipt` to return `{ status: 'reverted' }` and assert `reason: 'receipt_reverted'`. They exercise the same code path in `settle.ts` (lines 386-389: `if receipt.status === 'success'` else return `receipt_reverted`). The 'undecoded' scenario the spec envisions — where a thrown exception has no decodable reason string — would require `waitForTransactionReceipt` to throw an error object, not return a reverted receipt. The two tests differ only in name.
findings.details: The spec anchor says: 'receipt returns status === reverted but the revert reason cannot be decoded — classifier falls back to receipt_reverted.' This is about a WriteContract exception (where the classifier might try string-extract and fail), not a receipt object. A meaningful distinct test would mock `writeContract` to throw a `ContractFunctionExecutionError` whose cause has no shortMessage/metaMessages/reason string, and assert it falls back to `gas_estimate_revert` or `receipt_reverted` rather than `authorization_already_used_onchain`.

replacement.approach: Replace the duplicate 'undecoded' test with a throw-path test that has no decodable reason.

findings.location: settle.test.ts — 'WalletClient built from OpaqueRelayerKey — raw key not enumerable in errors'
findings.title: Raw key leak check serializes SettleResult, not the error or WalletClient state
findings.description: The test serializes `result` (a `{ ok: false, reason: 'gas_estimate_revert' }` object) and checks the raw key bytes do not appear in it. Since SettleResult carries only `ok`, `reason`, and optional `details`, the key can never appear in it regardless of implementation. The test would pass even if the raw key were appended to the `reason` string — only the exact field values are checked via `not.toContain`. A meaningful test would capture the caught error object or inspect module internals.
findings.details: The test is fulfilling the spec anchor letter but not its spirit. The real risk is the key leaking in a thrown error's `.message`, `.stack`, or a `console.log` of the opts/config. The result object is not the vector.

replacement.approach: Also serialize the error thrown by walletWriteSpy if it is rethrown/captured, or snapshot the stringified opts.

findings.title: Exact validBefore == nowSec+5 boundary (the <= edge) is not tested
findings.description: The spec tests nowSec+4 (fails) and nowSec+6 (passes). The implementation uses `validBeforeMs <= nowMs + SAFETY_MARGIN_MS` which means nowSec+5 also fails (5000 <= 5000 is true). The exact fence-post at nowSec+5 is the boundary between fail and pass, and it is untested.
findings.details: This is a low-severity gap; the existing +4/+6 tests already pin the operator direction. However fence-post tests at the exact boundary (equals case) are a standard item in the spec's testing strategy section and protect against a future operator flip from <= to <.

replacement.approach: Add a single test for validBefore at exactly nowSec+5.

positive_observations: All 14 verify TDD anchors and all 19 settle TDD anchors are present — no mandatory spec anchor is absent.
verify.test.ts uses real privateKeyToAccount + signTypedData for all four domain-tamper tests; no signature mocking means the EIP-712 recovery path is exercised end-to-end for every tamper variant.

settle.test.ts correctly imports WaitForTransactionReceiptTimeoutError and TimeoutError as distinct viem classes — the mine_timeout vs rpc_timeout distinction is explicitly tested and the viem mock passes actual error instances (not plain objects), so the instanceof checks in settle.ts are exercised.

The MIN_RELAYER_USDC_BALANCE constant is imported by name in the three proactive-balance tests — tests reference the module constant, not a hardcoded literal. This is the correct fixture-reuse pattern.
The static-grep approach for SecurityLogger and replay-store checks is sound: it strips block and line comments before matching, avoiding false positives from documentation text.
The chainId-pin caching test uses __resetSettleCacheForTests() in beforeEach — deterministic and does not rely on module isolation hacks.
CAIP-2/alias normalization test passes the payload network as 'eip155:5042002' while opts.expectedNetwork is 'arc-testnet', correctly testing the cross-form comparison path.
The 'duplicate nonce rejected' test calls makePayload() twice with the same underlying nonce fixture, confirming the NonceStore actually retains the entry across calls.

overall_assessment.branch_coverage: All spec-required branches are covered. Both sides of every conditional (valid/invalid signature, each domain field tamper, to_mismatch, value, validBefore/validAfter boundaries, nonce replay, all 7 settlement reasons, chainId pin pass/fail) have at least one test.
overall_assessment.table_driven_style: Four domain-tamper tests share the makePayload() fixture with domain-override pattern — this is good table-driven style. The settle error tests also share makeOpts() + walletWriteSpy. No concern here.
overall_assessment.mock_clarity: PublicClient is a plain object stub — correct for unit tests. WalletClient is intercepted via vi.mock('viem'). The mocking is appropriately scoped (2 external deps in verify, 3 in settle — within the 3-dep limit). The only clarity issue is finding T7-TEST-01 where the NonceStore spy is not wired to the call under test.
overall_assessment.fixture_reuse: Fixtures from T6 primitives (NETWORKS, NonceStore, OpaqueRelayerKey) are imported and used, not redefined. Good reuse.
required_actions: Fix T7-TEST-01 (medium): wire NonceStore spy through both verify and settle in the round-trip test.
Fix T7-TEST-02 (medium): replace microtask-pre scheduling with in-has-spy microtask queueing to make the synchrony proof robust.
optional_improvements: T7-TEST-03: replace the duplicate undecoded-revert test with a meaningful no-decodable-reason exception test.
T7-TEST-04: extend the OpaqueRelayerKey test to also check serialized opts, not only SettleResult.
T7-TEST-05: add the fence-post test at validBefore == nowSec+5.

task_required.reason: No critical or high severity issues. Both medium findings can be fixed in the current task context — they are targeted one- or two-line test corrections. The suite is functionally correct and all spec anchors are represented; the fixes improve structural robustness, not coverage count.
